content.problemStatement:
Pushpa has entered Chefland and wants to establish Pushpa-Raj here too.

Chefland has NNN towers where the height of the ithi^{th}ith tower is HiH_iHi‚Äã. To establish Pushpa-Raj, Pushpa does the following:


Initially, Pushpa chooses any tower iii (1‚â§i‚â§N)(1\le i \le N)(1‚â§i‚â§N) and lands on the roof of that tower.
Let XXX denote the height of the tower Pushpa is currently on, then, Pushpa can land on the roof of any tower jjj (1‚â§j‚â§N)(1\le j \le N)(1‚â§j‚â§N) such that the height of the jthj^{th}jth tower is (X+1)(X+1)(X+1).
Let iii denote the index of the tower on which Pushpa lands, then, the height of all towers **except** tower iii **increases** by 111 after each jump **including the initial jump**.

To establish Pushpa-Raj, Pushpa wants to land at the **maximum** height possible. Determine the maximum height Pushpa can reach.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the number of towers
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \dots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã denoting the initial heights of each of the towers from the ground.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the **maximum** height achieved by Pushpa.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: If Pushpa chooses to start from tower at index 111 with height of 111 unit, then, the new heights will be [1,3,2,4][ 1, 3, 2, 4 ][1,3,2,4]. He can then jump to the 3rd3^{rd}3rd tower (with height 222 units). The updated heights of the towers is [2,4,2,5][2, 4, 2, 5][2,4,2,5]. Pushpa is unable to make any more jumps.

The optimal way to in this case is to take an initial jump at tower 444 with height 333. The maximum height Pushpa can reach is 333.

Test case 222: There is only 111 tower so Pushpa reaches the maximum height of 222 units.
